question: Will Halcyon Analytics renew its 120k contract after the botched migration, or churn by the September renewal date?

halcyon_analytics.description: The vendor company whose contract is up for renewal.

customer_organization.description: The client company that holds the contract with Halcyon Analytics.

person.description: An individual actor in the scenario.

migration_project.description: The data migration project that was botched.

goodwill_credit.description: A financial credit offered by Halcyon to the customer as compensation.

contract_renewal_decision.description: The formal decision record about contract renewal.

contract_active.description: Whether the contract is currently active.

migration_botched.description: Whether the migration was considered botched by the customer.

communication_silence.description: Whether a person has gone silent (not responding to communications).

goodwill_credit_authorized.description: Whether a goodwill credit has been authorized by Nina.

renewal_decision_made.description: Whether a renewal decision has been formally made.

has_contract_with.description: Halcyon has a contract with the customer.

responsible_for_migration.description: Person or team responsible for the migration project.

day_to_day_access.description: Customer success lead has day-to-day access to customer engineers.

migration_outage_occurred.description: A two-day outage occurred during migration.

goodwill_credit_offered.description: Nina offers a goodwill credit to the customer.

renewal_decision_recorded.description: Marcus Webb records his renewal decision.

communication_breakdown.description: A person stops responding to communications.

unmodeled_actor_action.description: schema-scoped scaffolding: an actor action whose semantics could not be compiled; exact content preserved; counted as a fallback

renewal_decision_process.description: The process by which Marcus Webb decides to renew or churn.

goodwill_credit_process.description: Process for offering and accepting a goodwill credit.

renewal_decision_authority.description: Who holds the authority to decide renewal.
renewal_decision_authority.evidence: Renewal decision rests solely with Webb.

goodwill_credit_authority.description: Who can authorize a goodwill credit.
goodwill_credit_authority.evidence: A goodwill credit of up to 15k is within Nina's authority.

renewal_deadline.description: The contract renewal date is September 1, 2025.
renewal_deadline.constraint: renewal_decision must be made on or before 2025-09-01

credit_limit.description: Nina can authorize up to $15,000 goodwill credit.

marcus_webb_silence_rule.description: Marcus Webb has gone quiet; this is known to Nina and Tran.

tran_access_rule.description: Tran has day-to-day access to Webb's engineers.

decision_solely_webb_rule.description: Only Marcus Webb can make the renewal decision.

nina_petrova.why_consequential: She can authorize goodwill credit up to $15k to influence the renewal decision.

marcus_webb.why_consequential: He holds sole authority over the renewal decision; his team suffered the outage.

success_lead_tran.why_consequential: Has day-to-day access to Webb's engineers, can gather intelligence on sentiment.

unresolved_mechanisms: Whether Marcus Webb will accept any goodwill credit offered.
Whether the two-day outage is considered irreparable damage by Webb.
Whether Tran's access to engineers can surface actionable sentiment before the deadline.
assumptions: No other decision-makers exist for the renewal; Webb is the sole authority.
Nina has not yet offered a goodwill credit as of prediction time.
The renewal date is exactly September 1, 2025.

missing_decisive_elements: No representation of the customer's internal business impact (e.g., lost revenue, SLA penalties, reputational damage) which is the primary driver of churn, not just the botched migration itself.
No representation of competing vendor offers or market alternatives that Marcus Webb might be evaluating.
No representation of the personal relationship or trust history between Nina Petrova and Marcus Webb prior to the migration.
No representation of the customer's contractual exit penalties or switching costs that could anchor the decision.
relabeled_progress_bars: The 'goodwill_credit_accepted_and_satisfactory' trigger is a false progress bar — acceptance does not imply satisfaction; the credit could be accepted but deemed insultingly low, still driving churn.
The 'continued_silence_or_escalation' trigger conflates passive silence with active escalation, which are opposite signals in real negotiation dynamics.
The 'deadline_passed_without_decision' terminal state is a cop-out — it assumes indecision defaults to churn, but in practice many contracts auto-renew or roll over.
hidden_answer_risks: The model assumes goodwill credit is the only lever Nina has, ignoring escalation to higher management, personal apologies, or technical remediation offers.
The model treats Marcus Webb as a rational economic actor, but real renewal decisions are heavily influenced by internal politics, blame avoidance, and career risk after a public failure.
The model does not account for the possibility that the botched migration was actually caused by the customer's own data or requirements, shifting blame and reducing churn risk.
direct_human_reaction_encodings: No encoding of anger, frustration, or loss of trust as emotional states that can override rational cost-benefit analysis.
No encoding of 'face-saving' behavior — Marcus Webb may need to publicly punish Halcyon to demonstrate accountability to his own team or superiors, even if the credit is generous.
No encoding of fatigue or decision avoidance — after a stressful migration, the path of least resistance may be to do nothing (auto-renew) rather than actively churn.
public_posture_vs_private_reality_confla: The model treats 'communication_silence' as a fact, but silence could be strategic (buying time, negotiating leverage) rather than a signal of impending churn.

The model assumes 'goodwill_credit_offered' is a transparent event, but in practice such offers are often made privately with non-disclosure agreements, making the public posture different from the private settlement.
The 'renewal_decision_recorded' event assumes a single formal timestamp, but real decisions are often provisional, reversible, or made informally long before being recorded.
missing_nonhuman_mechanisms: No representation of automated contract renewal clauses or notice periods that could force a decision regardless of human sentiment.
No representation of the technical state of the migrated system post-botch (e.g., data corruption, performance degradation) which is a non-human factor that independently drives churn.
No representation of calendar or scheduling constraints (e.g., Marcus Webb's availability to make a decision before September, or competing projects that delay attention).
